description: All tasks required to deliver a fully functioning Vue.js 3 frontend with TypeScript, Pinia state management, Vue Router, Axios API layer, WebSocket real-time collaboration, and markdown editing/preview. Tasks are ordered by dependency — complete them in sequence unless marked as parallelizable.

tasks.name: Project Scaffold & Configuration
tasks.description: Initialize the frontend Vue.js 3 project with Vite and all required dependencies and configuration files. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/package.json` exists with all runtime dependencies (vue ^3.x, vue-router ^4.x, pinia ^2.x, axios ^1.x, marked or markdown-it for markdown rendering). (2) All dev dependencies are installed (vite, @vitejs/plugin-vue, typescript, vue-tsc). (3) `frontend/tsconfig.json` exists with strict mode enabled, targeting ESNext, module ESNext, moduleResolution bundler, jsx preserve, and paths configured for `@/*` alias to `src/*`. (4) `frontend/vite.config.ts` exists with the Vue plugin registered and server proxy or port configured (dev server on port 5173). (5) `frontend/src/main.ts` exists as the app entry point that creates the Vue app, registers Pinia and Vue Router, and mounts to `#app`. (6) `frontend/src/App.vue` exists as the root component containing a `<router-view />`. (7) `frontend/index.html` exists with the mount point `<div id="app">`. (8) `npm run dev` starts the Vite dev server without errors. (9) `npm run build` compiles without TypeScript errors. (10) `frontend/.gitignore` ignores node_modules and dist.

tasks.name: API Client with Auth Interceptors
tasks.description: Create the Axios API client singleton with request and response interceptors for JWT auth. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/api/client.ts` exports a default Axios instance with `baseURL: 'http://localhost:3000/api'`. (2) A request interceptor reads the access token from the auth store and sets the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header on every outgoing request. (3) A response interceptor catches 401 responses, attempts a token refresh via the auth store's `refresh()` action, and retries the original request with the new token. If refresh fails, it calls `auth.logout()` to clear state and redirect. (4) The interceptor avoids infinite retry loops — if the retried request also returns 401, it does not attempt another refresh. (5) The Axios instance is the single source for all API calls — no other Axios instances are created anywhere in the project.

tasks.name: Auth API Module
tasks.description: Create the API wrapper functions for all auth endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/api/auth.ts` exports four async functions: `registerUser(email, password)` calls POST /api/auth/register and returns { user, accessToken, refreshToken }; `loginUser(email, password)` calls POST /api/auth/login and returns the same shape; `refreshToken(refreshToken)` calls POST /api/auth/refresh and returns { accessToken, refreshToken }; `logoutUser(refreshToken)` calls POST /api/auth/logout. (2) All functions use the shared Axios client from `api/client.ts`. (3) All functions are properly typed with TypeScript interfaces for request and response shapes. (4) Error responses are not caught here — they propagate to the caller (store or component).

tasks.name: Auth Pinia Store
tasks.description: Implement the Pinia auth store managing user session state and token lifecycle. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/stores/auth.ts` exports a `useAuthStore` Pinia store using the setup store pattern (Composition API style). (2) State includes: `user` (reactive ref, `{ id: string, email: string } | null`, initially null), `accessToken` (reactive ref, `string | null`, stored in memory only — never in localStorage), `refreshToken` (reactive ref, `string | null`, synced with localStorage). (3) Computed getter `isAuthenticated` returns true when `user` is not null and `accessToken` is not null. (4) Action `login(email, password)`: calls the auth API's `loginUser`, stores the returned user, accessToken, and refreshToken (refreshToken also persisted to localStorage). (5) Action `register(email, password)`: calls `registerUser`, stores tokens and user same as login. (6) Action `logout()`: calls the auth API's `logoutUser` with the current refresh token, clears user/accessToken/refreshToken from state, removes refreshToken from localStorage. Logout must not throw even if the API call fails (catch and ignore). (7) Action `refresh()`: reads refreshToken from state (or localStorage as fallback), calls `refreshToken` API, updates accessToken and refreshToken in state and localStorage, returns true on success, returns false on failure (does not throw). (8) Action `initialize()`: called on app startup in main.ts — if a refreshToken exists in localStorage, attempts a refresh to restore the session; if refresh fails, clears state silently. (9) The store does NOT import or use `req`/`res` or any backend-specific types.

tasks.name: Vue Router with Auth Guards
tasks.description: Set up Vue Router with all route definitions and navigation guards for authentication. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/router/index.ts` exports a Vue Router instance using `createRouter` with `createWebHistory`. (2) Routes defined: `/login` -> LoginView (meta: { guest: true }), `/register` -> RegisterView (meta: { guest: true }), `/` -> DashboardView (meta: { requiresAuth: true }), `/notes/:id` -> NoteView (meta: { requiresAuth: true }). (3) A global `beforeEach` navigation guard checks: if the route requires auth (`meta.requiresAuth`) and the user is not authenticated (via auth store), redirect to `/login`; if the route is guest-only (`meta.guest`) and the user is authenticated, redirect to `/`. (4) The router is registered in `main.ts` via `app.use(router)`. (5) Navigating to an unknown route redirects to `/` (or shows a 404 page). (6) The `NoteView` route uses a dynamic param `:id` that is accessible via `useRoute().params.id`.

tasks.name: Login & Register Views
tasks.description: Implement the login and registration page components. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/views/LoginView.vue` uses `<script setup lang="ts">` and Composition API only. (2) LoginView contains a form with email input, password input, and submit button. (3) On submit, it calls `authStore.login(email, password)`. On success, navigates to `/`. On error, displays the error message from the API response (e.g. 'Invalid credentials', 'Missing required field'). (4) Shows a loading state while the login request is in progress (button disabled or spinner). (5) Has a link to `/register` for new users. (6) `frontend/src/views/RegisterView.vue` has the same structure but calls `authStore.register(email, password)`. (7) RegisterView validates that the password is at least 8 characters client-side before submitting. (8) RegisterView has a link to `/login`. (9) Both views redirect to `/` if the user is already authenticated (handled by router guard, but also defensively in the component). (10) Email and password inputs have appropriate HTML `type` attributes (`email`, `password`). (11) Forms handle Enter key submission.

tasks.name: Folders API Module
tasks.description: Create API wrapper functions for all folder endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/api/folders.ts` exports async functions: `fetchFolders()` calls GET /api/folders; `createFolder(name, parentId?)` calls POST /api/folders; `updateFolder(id, data)` calls PATCH /api/folders/:id; `deleteFolder(id)` calls DELETE /api/folders/:id; `fetchFolderShares(folderId)` calls GET /api/folders/:id/shares; `createFolderShare(folderId, email, permission)` calls POST /api/folders/:id/shares; `updateFolderShare(folderId, userId, permission)` calls PATCH /api/folders/:id/shares/:userId; `deleteFolderShare(folderId, userId)` calls DELETE /api/folders/:id/shares/:userId. (2) All functions are typed with proper TypeScript interfaces. (3) All use the shared Axios client.

tasks.name: Notes API Module
tasks.description: Create API wrapper functions for all note endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/api/notes.ts` exports async functions: `fetchNotes(folderId?)` calls GET /api/notes with optional query param; `createNote(title, content, folderId?)` calls POST /api/notes; `fetchNote(id)` calls GET /api/notes/:id; `updateNote(id, data)` calls PATCH /api/notes/:id; `deleteNote(id)` calls DELETE /api/notes/:id; `fetchNoteShares(noteId)` calls GET /api/notes/:id/shares; `createNoteShare(noteId, email, permission)` calls POST /api/notes/:id/shares; `updateNoteShare(noteId, userId, permission)` calls PATCH /api/notes/:id/shares/:userId; `deleteNoteShare(noteId, userId)` calls DELETE /api/notes/:id/shares/:userId. (2) All functions typed with TypeScript interfaces. (3) All use the shared Axios client.

tasks.name: Users API Module
tasks.description: Create API wrapper functions for user endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/api/users.ts` exports async functions: `fetchMe()` calls GET /api/users/me and returns { id, email, createdAt }; `searchUser(email)` calls GET /api/users/search?email=<email> and returns { id, email }. (2) Both functions are typed. (3) Both use the shared Axios client.

tasks.name: Folders Pinia Store
tasks.description: Implement the Pinia store for folder state management. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/stores/folders.ts` exports a `useFoldersStore` using the setup store pattern. (2) State: `folders` (reactive ref, array of folder objects with id, name, parentId, ownerId, createdAt, updatedAt, permission), `loading` (ref boolean), `error` (ref string | null). (3) Computed getter `rootFolders` returns folders where parentId is null. (4) Computed getter or method `getChildFolders(parentId)` returns folders whose parentId matches. (5) Action `loadFolders()`: calls `fetchFolders()` API, stores result in `folders`, manages loading/error state. (6) Action `addFolder(name, parentId?)`: calls `createFolder()` API, appends the returned folder to the `folders` array. (7) Action `editFolder(id, data)`: calls `updateFolder()` API, updates the folder in the local array. (8) Action `removeFolder(id)`: calls `deleteFolder()` API, removes the folder (and any children) from the local array. (9) All actions set `loading = true` before the API call and `loading = false` after (in finally block). (10) All actions catch errors and set the `error` state with the error message.

tasks.name: Notes Pinia Store
tasks.description: Implement the Pinia store for note state management. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/stores/notes.ts` exports a `useNotesStore` using the setup store pattern. (2) State: `notes` (reactive ref, array of note list items — id, title, folderId, ownerId, createdAt, updatedAt, permission — NO content), `currentNote` (reactive ref, full note object including content, or null), `loading` (ref boolean), `error` (ref string | null). (3) Action `loadNotes(folderId?)`: calls `fetchNotes()` API with optional filter, stores result in `notes`, manages loading/error. (4) Action `loadNote(id)`: calls `fetchNote()` API, stores result in `currentNote`. (5) Action `addNote(title, content, folderId?)`: calls `createNote()` API, appends to `notes` array, returns the created note. (6) Action `editNote(id, data)`: calls `updateNote()` API, updates the note in the `notes` array AND updates `currentNote` if it matches the id. (7) Action `removeNote(id)`: calls `deleteNote()` API, removes from `notes` array, clears `currentNote` if it was the deleted note. (8) Action `updateCurrentNoteContent(content)`: updates only the `currentNote.content` field locally (used for real-time collaboration where content comes from WebSocket, not REST). (9) All actions manage loading/error state correctly.

tasks.name: FolderTree Component
tasks.description: Implement the sidebar folder tree component for hierarchical folder navigation. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/components/FolderTree.vue` uses `<script setup lang="ts">`. (2) It renders the folder hierarchy as a tree structure, with root folders at the top level and child folders nested underneath. (3) Each folder item displays the folder name and can be clicked to select it (filtering notes by folderId). (4) A 'root' or 'All Notes' option is available to show unfiled notes or all notes. (5) Each folder owned by the current user shows action buttons/icons for: rename, delete, create subfolder, and share. (6) Emits events or calls store actions when: a folder is selected (emits `select` with folderId or null), a folder action is triggered. (7) The tree supports expand/collapse for folders with children. (8) A 'New Folder' button is available at the top level to create a root folder. (9) The component reads folder data from the folders store — does not make API calls directly. (10) Folders the user only has read access to are visually distinct (e.g. no delete/rename buttons, or a shared icon).

tasks.name: NoteCard Component
tasks.description: Implement the note list item card component. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/components/NoteCard.vue` uses `<script setup lang="ts">`. (2) Props: `note` (typed object with id, title, folderId, ownerId, createdAt, updatedAt, permission). (3) Displays the note title and last updated time (formatted as relative time or readable date). (4) Shows a visual indicator for the user's permission level (owner, write, read). (5) Clicking the card navigates to `/notes/:id` using the router. (6) If the user is the owner, shows a delete button/icon that emits a `delete` event with the note id. (7) The component does not make API calls directly — it only renders props and emits events.

tasks.name: DashboardView
tasks.description: Implement the main dashboard page that combines the folder sidebar and note list. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/views/DashboardView.vue` uses `<script setup lang="ts">`. (2) The layout has a sidebar containing the FolderTree component and a main content area showing the list of notes. (3) On mount, it loads folders (via folders store) and notes (via notes store). (4) When a folder is selected in the FolderTree, the notes list is filtered to show notes in that folder (calls `notesStore.loadNotes(folderId)` or filters locally). (5) A 'New Note' button creates a new note (prompts for title or uses a default) in the currently selected folder (or root if none selected), then navigates to `/notes/:id`. (6) Notes are rendered using the NoteCard component. (7) When a note card's delete event fires, the dashboard calls `notesStore.removeNote(id)` and refreshes the list. (8) Loading and error states from both stores are displayed to the user (loading spinner, error banner). (9) A logout button is visible that calls `authStore.logout()` and redirects to `/login`. (10) The current user's email is displayed somewhere in the header/nav area.

tasks.name: MarkdownEditor Component
tasks.description: Implement the markdown text editor component. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/components/MarkdownEditor.vue` uses `<script setup lang="ts">`. (2) Props: `modelValue` (string — the raw markdown content), `readonly` (boolean, default false). (3) Emits: `update:modelValue` (string) — enabling v-model usage. (4) Renders a `<textarea>` (or equivalent editable element) that displays and allows editing the raw markdown text. (5) When readonly is true, the textarea is disabled/non-editable. (6) The textarea fills its container and has appropriate styling (monospace font, reasonable padding). (7) The component emits `update:modelValue` on every input change (not debounced — debouncing is the caller's responsibility). (8) Supports tab key insertion (inserts a tab character or spaces instead of moving focus).

tasks.name: MarkdownPreview Component
tasks.description: Implement the rendered markdown preview component. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/components/MarkdownPreview.vue` uses `<script setup lang="ts">`. (2) Props: `content` (string — raw markdown to render). (3) Uses `marked` or `markdown-it` library to convert the raw markdown string to HTML. (4) Renders the HTML output safely using `v-html` with appropriate sanitization to prevent XSS (use DOMPurify or the library's built-in sanitization if available). (5) The preview updates reactively when the `content` prop changes. (6) Basic markdown features render correctly: headings (h1-h6), bold, italic, code blocks, inline code, links, lists (ordered and unordered), blockquotes, horizontal rules, images. (7) The preview container has appropriate CSS styling for readability (prose-like typography, spacing between elements).

tasks.name: NoteView with Editor & Preview
tasks.description: Implement the full note editing page combining editor and preview. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/views/NoteView.vue` uses `<script setup lang="ts">`. (2) On mount, it reads the note ID from the route params and calls `notesStore.loadNote(id)`. (3) The layout shows the MarkdownEditor on one side and MarkdownPreview on the other (side-by-side or toggle-able). (4) The editor is bound to the current note's content via v-model. (5) When the user types, the content updates in real-time in the preview. (6) The note title is displayed and editable (inline edit or input field). Editing the title calls `notesStore.editNote(id, { title })`. (7) Content changes are saved via `notesStore.editNote(id, { content })` — debounced at ~500ms to avoid excessive API calls. (8) If the user has only 'read' permission, the editor is in readonly mode and a visual indicator shows 'Read Only'. (9) If the user has 'write' or 'owner' permission, the editor is fully editable. (10) A 'Back' button or breadcrumb navigates back to the dashboard. (11) Loading and error states are handled (show spinner while loading, show error message if note not found or no access). (12) The note's permission level is displayed somewhere on the page.

tasks.name: ShareModal Component
tasks.description: Implement the sharing modal for notes and folders. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/components/ShareModal.vue` uses `<script setup lang="ts">`. (2) Props: `resourceType` ('note' | 'folder'), `resourceId` (string), `visible` (boolean). (3) Emits: `close`. (4) When opened, it fetches the current shares for the resource (calls the appropriate API: fetchNoteShares or fetchFolderShares). (5) Displays a list of current shares showing each user's email and their permission level (read/write). (6) Each share row has: a dropdown/toggle to change permission between read and write (calls updateNoteShare or updateFolderShare), a remove button to revoke access (calls deleteNoteShare or deleteFolderShare). (7) An 'Add Share' section with an email input and permission selector (read/write). On submit, calls createNoteShare or createFolderShare. Displays error messages (user not found, already shared, etc.). (8) The modal is only shown when `visible` is true and overlays the page. Clicking outside or pressing Escape closes it. (9) After any share operation (add/update/remove), the share list is refreshed. (10) Only the owner should see the share button that opens this modal — the component itself can be rendered by any caller but should display an error if the API returns 403.

tasks.name: Integrate Sharing into Dashboard & NoteView
tasks.description: Wire the ShareModal into the dashboard and note view so users can manage sharing. This task is complete when: (1) In DashboardView, each note card owned by the current user has a 'Share' button that opens the ShareModal with resourceType='note'. (2) In the FolderTree, each folder owned by the current user has a 'Share' action that opens the ShareModal with resourceType='folder'. (3) In NoteView, if the user is the owner, a 'Share' button in the header/toolbar opens the ShareModal. (4) The ShareModal is rendered once in each parent view (not duplicated per item) and reused by passing different resourceId props. (5) After sharing or unsharing, relevant store data is refreshed if needed (e.g. if permissions change affect the current view).

tasks.name: WebSocket Composable
tasks.description: Implement the core WebSocket connection composable. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/composables/useWebSocket.ts` exports a composable function `useWebSocket()`. (2) On call, it establishes a WebSocket connection to `ws://localhost:3000/ws?token=<accessToken>`, reading the access token from the auth store. (3) Exposes `send(event: string, payload: any)` — serializes { event, payload } as JSON and sends over the WebSocket. (4) Exposes `on(event: string, handler: (payload: any) => void)` — registers a handler for a specific event name. When a message arrives, it parses the JSON and dispatches to the matching handler by event name. (5) Exposes `off(event: string, handler)` to remove a handler. (6) Exposes a reactive `connected` ref (boolean) indicating the connection state. (7) Implements automatic reconnection on disconnect with exponential backoff (e.g. 1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s). (8) On reconnect, re-authenticates with the current access token (reads from auth store at reconnect time — token may have been refreshed). (9) Exposes a `disconnect()` function that closes the WebSocket and stops reconnection attempts. (10) Handles the case where the auth store has no token — does not attempt connection, or disconnects if token becomes null. (11) Invalid JSON messages from the server are silently ignored.

tasks.name: Note Collaboration Composable
tasks.description: Implement the note-specific real-time collaboration composable that wraps the WebSocket composable. This task is complete when: (1) `frontend/src/composables/useNoteCollaboration.ts` exports a composable function `useNoteCollaboration(noteId: Ref<string>)`. (2) When noteId changes (or on initial call), it sends a `note:join` event with { noteId } via the WebSocket composable. (3) When noteId changes to a different value, it first sends `note:leave` for the previous noteId, then `note:join` for the new one. (4) On unmount (via `onUnmounted`), it sends `note:leave` for the current noteId. (5) Listens for `note:joined` — updates the notes store's currentNote content with the received content, and exposes the collaborators list as a reactive ref. (6) Listens for `note:updated` — updates the notes store's currentNote content with the received content (calls `notesStore.updateCurrentNoteContent(content)`), ensuring the update does NOT trigger a re-send back to the server (guards against echo loops). (7) Listens for `note:presence` — updates the collaborators reactive ref. (8) Listens for `note:error` — exposes error state as a reactive ref. (9) Exposes a `sendUpdate(content: string)` function that sends `note:update` with { noteId, content } — this function should be debounced internally at ~300ms. (10) Exposes reactive refs: `collaborators` (string[]), `error` ({ code, message } | null), `isConnected` (boolean from the WebSocket composable).

tasks.name: Integrate Real-time Collaboration into NoteView
tasks.description: Wire the note collaboration composable into the NoteView so real-time editing works. This task is complete when: (1) NoteView uses `useNoteCollaboration(noteId)` composable. (2) When the user types in the editor, the content update is sent both to the REST API (debounced save) and to the WebSocket (debounced via the composable's `sendUpdate`). (3) When a `note:updated` event arrives from another collaborator, the editor content updates without disrupting the local user's cursor position (or at minimum, the content updates visibly). (4) The collaborators list is displayed in the NoteView UI (e.g. showing user IDs or emails of other active editors). (5) When a user navigates away from the note, `note:leave` is sent and the room is cleaned up. (6) If the WebSocket connection is lost, the user can still edit via REST API (graceful degradation). (7) A visual indicator shows whether the WebSocket is connected (e.g. a small dot or text like 'Live' / 'Offline'). (8) The note content received from the initial `note:joined` event is used as the starting content instead of (or merged with) the REST API fetch, to ensure the latest version is shown.

tasks.name: UI Polish & Responsive Layout
tasks.description: Apply global styles, layout improvements, and ensure the app is usable and visually coherent. This task is complete when: (1) A global CSS file (or scoped styles in App.vue) provides a consistent base: reset/normalize, font stack, color variables, spacing scale. (2) The DashboardView has a responsive layout — sidebar collapses on small screens (hamburger menu or drawer). (3) The NoteView editor/preview layout adapts: on larger screens, side-by-side; on smaller screens, tabbed or stacked. (4) All forms (login, register, share) have consistent styling: input borders, focus states, error states (red border/text), button styles. (5) Loading states use a consistent spinner or skeleton UI. (6) Error messages are displayed in a consistent manner (e.g. toast/banner). (7) The app has a clean, minimal look — no unstyled HTML elements visible. (8) All interactive elements have hover/focus states for accessibility. (9) The app is navigable via keyboard (tab order, Enter to submit forms).

tasks.name: App Initialization & Token Restore Flow
tasks.description: Ensure the app properly initializes on startup, restoring the user session if a refresh token exists. This task is complete when: (1) In `main.ts`, before mounting the app, `authStore.initialize()` is called and awaited. (2) If a refresh token is found in localStorage, the app attempts a token refresh. If successful, the user is logged in and can navigate to protected routes. If it fails, the refresh token is cleared and the user is redirected to /login. (3) While initialization is in progress, the app shows a loading state (e.g. a full-screen spinner) instead of briefly flashing the login page. (4) The router guards work correctly after initialization — they check the auth store state which has been populated by the initialize flow. (5) If no refresh token is in localStorage, initialization completes immediately and the user sees the login page.

tasks.name: Error Handling & Edge Cases
tasks.description: Ensure all error scenarios are handled gracefully across the app. This task is complete when: (1) Network errors (server unreachable) display a user-friendly message rather than a raw error. (2) 403 responses on notes/folders show 'You do not have access' and offer navigation back to the dashboard. (3) 404 responses show 'Not found' with a back link. (4) Token expiry during active use (e.g. mid-edit) triggers a silent refresh — if refresh succeeds, the operation is retried transparently. If refresh fails, the user is redirected to login with a message. (5) WebSocket disconnection during editing does not lose unsaved content — the latest content is held in the store. (6) Creating a note or folder while offline/disconnected shows an error without crashing. (7) Double-clicking submit buttons does not create duplicate resources (disable button while loading). (8) All `async` actions in stores have try/catch blocks and set error state on failure.

tasks.name: Build Verification & Final Cleanup
tasks.description: Final validation that the entire frontend builds, runs, and integrates with the backend correctly. This task is complete when: (1) `npm run build` in the frontend directory completes with zero TypeScript errors and zero warnings. (2) `npm run dev` starts the dev server without errors. (3) No `any` types are used anywhere in the codebase unless explicitly justified. (4) No `console.log` debug statements remain in production code. (5) All components use `<script setup lang="ts">` and Composition API — no Options API usage. (6) All API calls go through the Pinia stores — no direct API calls from components. (7) All environment-specific values (API base URL, WS URL) are configurable and not hardcoded (use Vite env variables or a config file). (8) A manual walkthrough of the full user flow works end-to-end with the backend: register -> login -> create folder -> create note -> edit note with markdown preview -> share note with second user -> second user logs in and sees the shared note -> second user opens the note and sees real-time updates -> logout -> login again with restored session. (9) The frontend/tasks.json is updated with all tasks marked completed.
